meta.description: Rigorous analysis, original research, and grounded perspective across ten pillars of national renewal — from governance and justice to culture and ecology.
Rigorous analysis, original research, and grounded perspective across ten pillars of national renewal — from governance and justice to culture and ecology.

articles.title: Column: Conversion, Caste & Constitution
articles.summary: On March 24, 2026, a Division Bench of the Supreme Court of India comprising Justices Prashant Kumar Mishra and Manmohan pronounced a judgment that has defined the relationship between religious conversion, caste identity, and statutory protection under the Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes (Prevention of Atrocities) Act, 1989. Chinthada Anand v. State of Andhra Pradesh (Criminal Appeal No. 1580 of 2026) deserves a careful study, not merely for its doctrinal clarity but for the constitutional grammar it articulates.
articles.author: Adv. Aditya Kashyap

articles.title: Supreme Court's Tamil Nadu Governor verdict is judicial zeal gone too far
articles.summary: A critical appraisal of the Supreme Court's judgment on the Tamil Nadu Governor controversy — where the Court's reach may have exceeded its constitutional remit.
articles.author: Adv. Aditya Kashyap

articles.title: Spare The Judiciary: Don't Politicise Impeachment
articles.summary: The impeachment process is a solemn constitutional remedy — not a political instrument. A warning against weaponising it for institutional score-settling.
articles.author: Adv. Aditya Kashyap

articles.title: Rule Of Locus Vs Rule Of Law
articles.summary: When courts loosen the requirements of locus standi, do they strengthen access to justice — or weaken the integrity of adjudication? A doctrinal examination.
articles.author: Adv. Aditya Kashyap

articles.title: Bharat Mata: Living Mother Of The Constitution
articles.summary: The controversy over Bharat Mata's image at official functions reveals a deeper civilisational anxiety — and an opportunity to reclaim constitutional identity.
articles.author: Adv. Aditya Kashyap

articles.title: Don't Internationalise Terror, Neutralise It!
articles.summary: In the aftermath of Pahalgam, voices calling for internationalisation of the response miss the point. India's sovereign answer must be swift, certain and internal.
articles.author: Adv. Aditya Kashyap

articles.title: Revisiting Constitutional & Institutional Dharma: Need of the Hour
articles.summary: The Vice President's critique of the Supreme Court opens a larger conversation about the constitutional dharma that binds all three branches of government.
articles.author: Adv. Aditya Kashyap

articles.title: Religious Reservation: Fraud on the Constitution
articles.summary: Reservations premised on religious identity strike at the secular foundations of the Constitution. A legal argument for why such schemes must not survive judicial scrutiny.
articles.author: Adv. Aditya Kashyap

articles.title: In Defense of "Justice"
articles.summary: When a sitting judge speaks on the Uniform Civil Code, the reaction reveals more about India's legal culture than the speech itself. A defence of open constitutional discourse.
articles.author: Adv. Aditya Kashyap

articles.title: Unravelling The Quest in Courts for Ram Mandir
articles.summary: A detailed legal history of the Ram Janmabhoomi litigation — the evidence, the arguments, and what the Supreme Court's verdict ultimately settled and left open.
articles.author: Adv. Aditya Kashyap

articles.title: Victim-Centric Justice in New Criminal Laws
articles.summary: The Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita's most significant departure from its colonial predecessor is its centering of the victim. What this shift means in practice.
articles.author: Adv. Aditya Kashyap

articles.title: Liberating Justice from Colonial Legacy: Decoding Nyaya, Suraksha & Sakshya Bills
articles.summary: A comprehensive analysis of India's new criminal law trilogy — what changes, what continues, and whether the decolonisation project goes far enough.
articles.author: Adv. Aditya Kashyap

articles.title: Key Changes in the Law of Evidence: Bharatiya Sakshya Adhiniyam, 2023
articles.summary: From electronic records to witness protection — a section-by-section breakdown of what India's new evidence law adds, removes, and clarifies.
articles.author: Adv. Aditya Kashyap

articles.title: Malegaon Blast Case: Vindication beyond doubt
articles.summary: The acquittals in the Malegaon blast case are not merely a legal outcome — they are a reckoning with how prosecutorial overreach and political motivation distort justice.
articles.author: Adv. Aditya Kashyap

articles.title: 1975 Emergency: Democratic constitution vs Dynastic leadership
articles.summary: Fifty years on, the Emergency remains the darkest chapter in India's constitutional history. Its lessons for today's democracy are more urgent than ever.
articles.author: Adv. Aditya Kashyap

articles.title: Special Intensive Revision in Bihar: Agitating for Illegals
articles.summary: The political mobilisation against Bihar's electoral roll revision exposes a disturbing pattern of advocacy for those who have no legal right to be on the rolls.
articles.author: Adv. Aditya Kashyap

articles.title: Right to Reside Exclusive to Citizens and not to Illegal Immigrants
articles.summary: The Supreme Court's ruling securing India from illegal Rohingyas reaffirms a foundational constitutional principle: territorial rights belong to citizens, not migrants.
articles.author: Adv. Aditya Kashyap

articles.title: Beyond Sambhal: Truth and Reconciliation Healing the Historical Wounds
articles.summary: Sambhal is not an isolated incident but a symptom of unresolved historical wounds. True healing requires acknowledgment, not erasure.
articles.author: Adv. Aditya Kashyap

articles.title: Muslim Women Maintenance: Justice for Shahbano's Sisters
articles.summary: Forty years after Shah Bano, the Supreme Court's ruling on Muslim women's maintenance rights closes a circle of injustice that politics once forced open.
articles.author: Adv. Aditya Kashyap

articles.title: Motivated and Malicious: V K Saxena vs Medha Patkar Defamation
articles.summary: The defamation verdict against Medha Patkar exposes how civil society activism can slide into motivated and malicious personal attacks — and the courts' role in checking that.
articles.author: Adv. Aditya Kashyap

articles.title: Mushrooming of Minority Institutions: A Constitutional Fraud
articles.summary: The proliferation of institutions claiming minority status to escape regulatory accountability represents a systematic abuse of Article 30 — and demands judicial correction.
articles.author: Adv. Aditya Kashyap

articles.title: Reclaiming Sacred Spaces in Kashi and Mathura: A Legal Odyssey
articles.summary: The Gyanvapi litigation is not merely a property dispute — it is a civilisational question about whether India's legal system can honestly confront its historical legacy.
articles.author: Adv. Aditya Kashyap

articles.title: Article 370 verdict: A glorious spot in the annals of Bharat's Judiciary
articles.summary: The Supreme Court's unanimous upholding of the abrogation of Article 370 is a landmark that will define the constitutional jurisprudence of national integration for decades.
articles.author: Adv. Aditya Kashyap

articles.title: The Legal Labyrinth: Verdict on Same-Sex Union
articles.summary: The Supreme Court's refusal to recognise same-sex marriage navigates a complex labyrinth of constitutional interpretation, legislative competence and social change.
articles.author: Adv. Aditya Kashyap

articles.title: Cover Story — Criminal Justice System: Curating the Nyaya, Suraksha and Sakshya Bills
articles.summary: A comprehensive cover story on India's new criminal law framework — the philosophy behind the bills, the debates they sparked, and what genuine reform requires.
articles.author: Adv. Aditya Kashyap

articles.title: One Nation One Election: Re-booting the Election Cycle
articles.summary: The case for simultaneous elections goes beyond saving money — it is about restoring governance continuity and ending the permanent campaign mode that paralyses policy.
articles.author: Adv. Aditya Kashyap

articles.title: National Youth Day: Young Aspirations & The Way Ahead
articles.summary: On Swami Vivekananda's birth anniversary, a reflection on what India's youth owe to the nation — and what the nation owes to its youth.
articles.author: Adv. Aditya Kashyap
